body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index plummeted to 7,460.60 at the opening bell and remained volatile throughout the morning, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, and IDX80 also experiencing declines. Meanwhile, some blue-chip stocks like PT Barito Pacific and PT Bank Central Asia saw declines, while others like PT Adaro Energy and PT Vale Indonesia experienced improvements.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Fluctuates Sharply Amid Mixed Performance of Blue-Chip Stocks

body: PT BPD Jawa Timur TBK will acquire 476.19 million shares of PT BPD Banten TBK, with an investment of IDR 10 billion, as part of its plan to enter the banking business group. The acquisition is valued at IDR 22 per share, with the highest price recorded in September 2024 at IDR 27 per share, and PT BPD Banten TBK having a total of 51.87 billion shares, with 66.11% owned by controllers and 33.89% by non-controllers.
title: PT BPD Jawa Timur to Acquire 476 Million Shares of PT BPD Banten for IDR 10 Billion

body: PT Rig Tenders Indonesia's subsidiary, PT Batuah Abadi Lines, has acquired a new vessel, Tug Boat PEC 2416, which is expected to positively impact the company's operational and financial conditions, increasing assets and revenue to meet growing demand. As of June 30, 2024, PT Rig Tenders Indonesia reported cash and cash equivalents of IDR 267.02 billion, total assets of IDR 774.12 billion, and total equity of IDR 758.20 billion.
title: PT Rig Tenders Indonesia's subsidiary acquires new tug boat vessel to boost operational and financial performance.

body: PT Pelayaran Tamarin Samudra Tbk, an oil and gas supporting service provider, has divested its subsidiary PT Pertamina Trans Kontinental for USD 13.79 million. As of June 30, 2024, the company reported total assets of USD 56.93 million and total equity of USD 33.98 million.
title: PT Pelayaran Tamarin Samudra Divests Vessel Unit to PT Pertamina Trans Kontinental for USD 13.79 Million

body: PT Sepatu Bata Tbk, an Indonesian footwear manufacturer, reported a loss of IDR 127.35 billion in the first half of 2024, a nearly threefold increase from the same period last year, due to low sales and increased expenses. The company's sales plummeted 22.5% year-on-year to IDR 260.29 billion, while expenses rose, including selling and marketing expenses, restructuring costs, and finance costs, but the company has initiated a performance recovery plan, including divesting assets and winding down its factory.
title: Bata Indonesia reports significant loss in first half of 2024 due to low sales and increased expenses.
